evals.prompt: List all Tier 1 hooks for the LP Craft pillar on LinkedIn: `python3 scripts/hook_lib.py list --tier 1 --pillar lp-craft --channel linkedin`
evals.expected_output: CLI returns a tabular list of Tier 1 hooks where pillar fit includes lp-craft and channel fit includes linkedin. Each row shows hook id, name, category, last_used_at. Empty tier returns 'No Tier 1 hooks for lp-craft on linkedin yet — falling back to Tier 2'.

assertions.name: filters_applied
assertions.description: Output only contains hooks where tier=1 AND pillar fit includes lp-craft AND channel fit includes linkedin

assertions.name: tabular_format
assertions.description: Each row shows id, name, category, last_used_at columns

assertions.name: empty_state_handled
assertions.description: When 0 Tier 1 hooks match, returns clear fallback message instead of empty output

assertions.name: tier_cap_enforced
assertions.description: Output never exceeds 8 Tier 1 hooks per pillar (architectural cap)

evals.prompt: Promote the brutal-truth-text-post hook to Tier 1: `python3 scripts/hook_lib.py promote brutal-truth-text-post --reason '4 HITs in last 28d (LI, +220% vs baseline)'`
evals.expected_output: Script updates data/hooks.json (tier 2→1) AND appends an entry to data/promotion-log.json with timestamp + reason + source. If pillar already has 8 Tier 1 hooks, refuses promotion and instructs user to demote one first.

assertions.name: tier_field_updated
assertions.description: data/hooks.json: brutal-truth-text-post tier changes from 2 to 1

assertions.name: promotion_log_entry
assertions.description: data/promotion-log.json gains an entry with hook_id, from_tier, to_tier, timestamp, reason, source

assertions.name: tier_cap_enforced
assertions.description: If pillar Tier 1 cap (8) would be exceeded, promotion is refused with message 'demote a Tier 1 hook first'

assertions.name: audit_trail_immutable
assertions.description: Promotion log entries are append-only — existing entries never modified
